req.title: Open savings account (negative variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-052 defines a savings-account creation endpoint with a hard floor of $100 initial deposit and automatic tier assignment. The test strategy covers: (1) EP positive path — a well-formed request with a valid balance well above the floor, confirming 201 + tier assignment; (2) NEG path — balance clearly below the floor (e.g. $50), expecting 422 with a machine-readable error code; (3) BVA boundary — balance exactly at the floor ($100), the minimum acceptable value per the spec; (4) BVA off-by-one — balance one cent below the floor ($99.99), the upper invalid value; (5) NEG unauthorised — missing/invalid auth token, expecting 401; (6) DT combination — multiple invalid fields simultaneously, confirming the API surfaces all validation errors in one response. Each test is idempotent because it relies on a per-test ephemeral customer fixture that is torn down after the test, and no shared state is mutated between tests.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_balance_above_minimum
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: EP: representative value from valid-deposit equivalence class (>= 100)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_valid_balance_above_minimum(verified_customer, auth_headers, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers(verified_customer)
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("interest_tier") is not None
    assert float(body["balance"]) == 500.00

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_insufficient_funds_returns_422
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: NEG: invalid partition — deposit below mandatory minimum
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_insufficient_funds_returns_422(verified_customer, auth_headers, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 50.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers(verified_customer)
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_INITIAL_DEPOSIT"
    assert "account_id" not in body

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_one_cent_below_minimum_rejected
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: BVA: off-by-one below lower boundary — $99.99 must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_one_cent_below_minimum_rejected(verified_customer, auth_headers, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 99.99,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers(verified_customer)
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_INITIAL_DEPOSIT"
    assert "account_id" not in body

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_unauthorised_request_returns_401
preconditions: No auth token provided (or token is invalid/expired)
actions: POST /accounts/savings with body {"customer_id": <customer_id>, "initial_deposit": 500.00} and no Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: NEG: missing credentials must be rejected before business logic executes
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_unauthorised_request_returns_401(verified_customer, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings",
        json=payload
        # deliberately omit Authorization header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" not in body

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_multiple_invalid_fields_all_errors_returned
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: response body errors list contains entry for initial_deposit
response body errors list contains entry for currency
trace: DT: combination of multiple invalid inputs — API must surface all validation failures simultaneously
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_multiple_invalid_fields_all_errors_returned(verified_customer, auth_headers, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 0
        # currency intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers(verified_customer)
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_fields = [e.get("field") for e in body.get("errors", [])]
    assert "initial_deposit" in error_fields
    assert "currency" in error_fields
    assert "account_id" not in body

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_tier_transitions_across_balance_thresholds
preconditions: API exposes at least two distinct interest tiers based on deposit amount
actions: POST /accounts/savings with initial_deposit = 100.00; record interest_tier as tier_low
POST /accounts/savings with initial_deposit = 10000.00; record interest_tier as tier_high
expected: tier_low and tier_high are different values
trace: R-FIN-052: tiered interest rates applied automatically
STC: state transition — system moves to different tier-state depending on deposit amount
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_tier_transitions_across_balance_thresholds(verified_customer, auth_headers, api_base_url):
    base = f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings"
    headers = auth_headers(verified_customer)
    cid = verified_customer["id"]

    resp_low = requests.post(base, json={"customer_id": cid, "initial_deposit": 100.00, "currency": "USD"}, headers=headers)
    assert resp_low.status_code == 201, resp_low.text

    resp_high = requests.post(base, json={"customer_id": cid, "initial_deposit": 10000.00, "currency": "USD"}, headers=headers)
    assert resp_high.status_code == 201, resp_high.text

    tier_low = resp_low.json()["interest_tier"]
    tier_high = resp_high.json()["interest_tier"]
    assert tier_low != tier_high, f"Expected different tiers; both returned {tier_low!r}"
    assert resp_low.json()["account_id"] != resp_high.json()["account_id"]
